docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/artifacts/story-08/001-implementor.jsonl:22:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_11","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,500p' docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/tech-design-cli-runtime.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Technical Design Companion: CLI Runtime Surface\n\n## Purpose\n\nThis companion covers the `ls-impl-cli` runtime that executes bounded operations on behalf of the `ls-claude-impl` orchestrator. The CLI is not the methodology. It is the execution surface that makes the methodology practical. It validates readiness, assembles provider-facing prompts, calls secondary harnesses when configured, captures structured outputs, and returns disciplined result contracts to the orchestrator.\n\nThe design goal is an agent-first CLI. Every command should correspond to a clear process action that the orchestrator already understands from the skill docs. The CLI should hide internal complexity but not produce mystery. It should accept explicit inputs, return explicit results, and avoid holding orchestration lifecycle in hidden runtime state between calls.\n\n## Runtime Surface Overview\n\nThe runtime has five responsibilities:\n\n1. Resolve and validate the spec-pack surface.\n2. Load and validate the orchestrator-owned run config.\n3. Assemble prompts from embedded base prompts, snippets, runtime values, and public inserts.\n4. Execute one bounded operation through the appropriate primary/secondary harness combination.\n5. Return structured results that the orchestrator can persist and act on.\n\nThe runtime does **not** own:\n\n- story progression\n- acceptance decisions\n- cumulative baselines across the whole run\n- cleanup review decisions\n- epic closeout decisions\n\nThose remain with the orchestrator.\n\n## Shared Result Envelope\n\nEvery command should return the same outer JSON envelope so the orchestrator can ingest results without command-specific parsing rules.\n\n```typescript\nexport interface CliError {\n  code: string;\n  message: string;\n  detail?: string;\n}\n\nexport interface CliArtifactRef {\n  kind: string;\n  path: string;\n}\n\nexport interface CliResultEnvelope<T> {\n  command: string;\n  version: 1;\n  status: \"ok\" | \"needs-user-decision\" | \"blocked\" | \"error\";\n  outcome: string;\n  result?: T;\n  errors: CliError[];\n  warnings: string[];\n  artifacts: CliArtifactRef[];\n  startedAt: string;\n  finishedAt: string;\n}\n```\n\nThe JSON envelope is the source of truth. Human-readable `stderr` is allowed for progress or debugging, but the orchestrator should route decisions from the envelope rather than from ad hoc terminal text.\n\nSuggested stable error codes:\n\n- `INVALID_SPEC_PACK`\n- `INVALID_RUN_CONFIG`\n- `VERIFICATION_GATE_UNRESOLVED`\n- `PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE`\n- `PROVIDER_OUTPUT_INVALID`\n- `CONTINUATION_HANDLE_INVALID`\n- `PROMPT_ASSET_MISSING`\n- `PROMPT_INSERT_INVALID`\n\n## Public CLI Contract\n\nThe public CLI contract consists of:\n\n1. command signatures in `Command Signatures`\n2. shared JSON envelope in `Shared Result Envelope`\n3. stdout/stderr/artifact behavior in `Public CLI IO Contract`\n4. exit-code and routing behavior in `Exit Codes` and `Result Routing Matrix`\n5. provider execution assumptions in `Provider Invocation Contracts`\n\nAn implementation that matches those five surfaces should be interchangeable at the orchestrator boundary even if internal module structure changes later.\n\n## Command Surface\n\nThe public CLI surface should be small and stable.\n\n| Command | Purpose | Required Inputs | Primary Outputs | Outcome States |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `inspect` | Resolve spec-pack layout and story inventory | spec-pack root | spec-pack summary, story order, insert presence | `ready`, `needs-user-decision`, `blocked` |\n| `preflight` | Validate run config, provider availability, prompt assets, and run readiness | spec-pack root, `impl-run.config.json` | readiness report, validated config, provider matrix | `ready`, `needs-user-decision`, `blocked` |\n| `story-implement` | Start implementation for one story | story id, spec-pack root, run config | implementor result, continuation handle | `ready-for-verification`, `needs-followup-fix`, `needs-human-ruling`, `blocked` |\n| `story-continue` | Continue a retained implementor session for the current story | continuation handle, follow-up request | continued implementor result | `ready-for-verification`, `needs-followup-fix`, `needs-human-ruling`, `blocked` |\n| `story-verify` | Run a fresh verifier batch for one story | story id, run config, verification context | verifier batch result array | `pass`, `revise`, `block` |\n| `quick-fix` | Apply a narrow, bounded fix request | fix request, affected scope, role config | quick-fix result | `ready-for-verification`, `needs-more-routing`, `blocked` |\n| `epic-cleanup` | Apply cleanup-only corrections before epic verification | cleanup batch, selected fix workflow | cleanup result | `cleaned`, `needs-more-cleanup`, `blocked` |\n| `epic-verify` | Run fresh epic-level verification | spec-pack root, run config | epic verifier result array | `pass`, `revise`, `block` |\n| `epic-synthesize` | Verify and consolidate epic-level findings | epic verifier results, run config | synthesis result | `ready-for-closeout`, `needs-fixes`, `needs-more-verification`, `blocked` |\n\nThe CLI with no arguments should print quick-start help only. The full process explanation stays in the skill docs.\n\n### Command Signatures\n\nThe public signatures should stay stable even if internal implementation changes.\n\n| Command | Signature |\n|---|---|\n| `inspect` | `ls-impl-cli inspect --spec-pack-root <path> [--json]` |\n| `preflight` | `ls-impl-cli preflight --spec-pack-root <path> [--config <path>] [--story-gate <cmd>] [--epic-gate <cmd>] [--json]` |\n| `story-implement` | `ls-impl-cli story-implement --spec-pack-root <path> --story-id <id> [--config <path>] [--json]` |\n| `story-continue` | `ls-impl-cli story-continue --spec-pack-root <path> --story-id <id> --provider <provider> --session-id <id> [--config <path>] [--followup-file <path> | --followup-text <text>] [--json]` |\n| `story-verify` | `ls-impl-cli story-verify --spec-pack-root <path> --story-id <id> [--config <path>] [--json]` |\n| `quick-fix` | `ls-impl-cli quick-fix --spec-pack-root <path> --story-id <id> --request-file <path> [--scope-file <path>] [--config <path>] [--json]` |\n| `epic-cleanup` | `ls-impl-cli epic-cleanup --spec-pack-root <path> --cleanup-batch <path> [--config <path>] [--json]` |\n| `epic-verify` | `ls-impl-cli epic-verify --spec-pack-root <path> [--config <path>] [--json]` |\n| `epic-synthesize` | `ls-impl-cli epic-synthesize --spec-pack-root <path> --verifier-report <path> --verifier-report <path> [--config <path>] [--json]` |\n\nAll commands should support `--json` for machine-readable output. Human-readable summaries may exist for manual use, but the orchestrator contract should be the JSON payload.\n\n### Public CLI IO Contract\n\nThe CLI should behave consistently across commands.\n\n| Surface | Rule |\n|---|---|\n| stdout with `--json` | emit exactly one `CliResultEnvelope<T>` JSON object |\n| stdout without `--json` | emit a concise human-readable summary only |\n| stderr | reserved for progress/debugging; never required for orchestration routing |\n| persisted artifacts | write the same JSON envelope that was emitted on stdout |\n| default config path | `<spec-pack-root>/impl-run.config.json` unless `--config` overrides it |\n| default artifact root | `<spec-pack-root>/artifacts/` |\n\n### Command Examples\n\n```bash\nnode bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs inspect --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --json\nnode bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs preflight --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --json\nnode bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs story-implement --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --story-id story-03 --json\nnode bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs story-verify --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --story-id story-03 --json\nnode bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs epic-cleanup --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --cleanup-batch /abs/spec-pack/artifacts/cleanup/cleanup-batch.md --json\n```\n\nFor commands whose inner payload also contains an `outcome`, the envelope `outcome` is the routing source of truth and must match the inner payload exactly. Schema validation should reject mismatches.\n\n### Exit Codes\n\nExit codes are a coarse routing signal. The JSON payload remains the source of truth.\n\n| Exit Code | Meaning |\n|---|---|\n| `0` | Command completed and produced a non-blocking outcome (`ready`, `ready-for-verification`, `pass`, `cleaned`, `ready-for-closeout`) |\n| `2` | Command completed but requires follow-up (`needs-user-decision`, `needs-followup-fix`, `needs-human-ruling`, `revise`, `needs-more-routing`, `needs-more-cleanup`, `needs-fixes`, `needs-more-verification`) |\n| `3` | Command is blocked by environment, spec-pack, config, or provider readiness issue |\n| `1` | Invalid invocation, schema parse failure, or uncaught runtime error |\n\n### Result Routing Matrix\n\nThis matrix is authoritative for handler implementation and tests.\n\n| Command Outcome | Envelope Status | Exit Code | Orchestrator Action |\n|---|---|---:|---|\n| `ready` | `ok` | 0 | proceed to next setup step |\n| `ready-for-verification` | `ok` | 0 | run verification or next bounded step |\n| `pass` | `ok` | 0 | evaluate for acceptance or next stage |\n| `cleaned` | `ok` | 0 | move into epic verification |\n| `ready-for-closeout` | `ok` | 0 | run final orchestrator-owned gate |\n| `needs-user-decision` | `needs-user-decision` | 2 | pause and request human clarification |\n| `needs-followup-fix` | `ok` | 2 | route same-session follow-up or fix path |\n| `needs-human-ruling` | `needs-user-decision` | 2 | pause for orchestrator/human ruling |\n| `revise` | `ok` | 2 | route fix and rerun verification |\n| `needs-more-routing` | `ok` | 2 | select another bounded correction path |\n| `needs-more-cleanup` | `ok` | 2 | continue cleanup cycle |\n| `needs-fixes` | `ok` | 2 | route fixes before closeout |\n| `needs-more-verification` | `ok` | 2 | launch additional verification/synthesis |\n| `blocked` | `blocked` | 3 | stop and inspect blocking error details |\n| parse/schema/runtime failure | `error` | 1 | treat as CLI failure rather than feature-state outcome |\n\n## Runtime Module Layout\n\n```text\nprocesses/impl-cli/\n├── cli.ts\n├── commands/\n│   ├── inspect.ts\n│   ├── preflight.ts\n│   ├── story-implement.ts\n│   ├── story-continue.ts\n│   ├── story-verify.ts\n│   ├── quick-fix.ts\n│   ├── epic-cleanup.ts\n│   ├── epic-verify.ts\n│   └── epic-synthesize.ts\n├── core/\n│   ├── config-schema.ts\n│   ├── spec-pack.ts\n│   ├── story-order.ts\n│   ├── prompt-assets.ts\n│   ├── prompt-assembly.ts\n│   ├── provider-checks.ts\n│   ├── provider-adapters/\n│   │   ├── claude-code.ts\n│   │   ├── codex.ts\n│   │   └── copilot.ts\n│   ├── result-contracts.ts\n│   ├── status-codes.ts\n│   ├── command-context.ts\n│   └── embedded-assets.generated.ts\n└── prompts/\n    ├── base/\n    └── snippets/\n```\n\nThe command modules stay thin. They parse command-specific flags with `citty`, call into core services, and print structured JSON. All real behavior lives in `core/`.\n\n### Core Service Interfaces\n\nThe command handlers should depend on a small set of explicit core interfaces.\n\n```typescript\nexport interface SpecPackResolver {\n  resolve(specPackRoot: string): Promise<InspectResult>;\n}\n\nexport interface VerificationGateResolver {\n  resolve(input: {\n    specPackRoot: string;\n    explicitStoryGate?: string;\n    explicitEpicGate?: string;\n  }): Promise<VerificationGateConfig | CliError>;\n}\n\nexport interface ArtifactWriter {\n  write<T>(params: {\n    specPackRoot: string;\n    command: string;\n    storyId?: string;\n    payload: CliResultEnvelope<T>;\n  }): Promise<CliArtifactRef>;\n}\n\nexport interface ProviderResultParser<T> {\n  parse(raw: ProviderExecutionResult): Promise<T | CliError>;\n}\n```\n\nThese interfaces are deliberately smaller than the whole runtime. They pin the boundaries that are most likely to drift during implementation.\n\n## Run Configuration Contract\n\nThe CLI consumes an explicit orchestrator-owned JSON file. The contract is intentionally lean.\n\n```typescript\nexport type PrimaryHarness = \"claude-code\";\nexport type SecondaryHarness = \"codex\" | \"copilot\" | \"none\";\nexport type ReasoningEffort = \"low\" | \"medium\" | \"high\" | \"xhigh\";\n\nexport interface RoleAssignment {\n  secondary_harness: SecondaryHarness;\n  model: string;\n  reasoning_effort: ReasoningEffort;\n}\n\nexport interface EpicVerifierAssignment extends RoleAssignment {\n  label: string;\n}\n\nexport interface ImplRunConfig {\n  version: 1;\n  primary_harness: PrimaryHarness;\n  story_implementor: RoleAssignment;\n  quick_fixer: RoleAssignment;\n  story_verifier_1: RoleAssignment;\n  story_verifier_2: RoleAssignment;\n  self_review: {\n    passes: number;\n  };\n  epic_verifiers: EpicVerifierAssignment[];\n  epic_synthesizer: RoleAssignment;\n}\n```\n\nCanonical display labels:\n\n| Stored Value | Display Label |\n|---|---|\n| `low` | low |\n| `medium` | medium |\n| `high` | high |\n| `xhigh` | extra high |\n\n`c12` should be used in explicit-file mode for this feature. The CLI should load either the path passed via `--config` or the default `impl-run.config.json` in the spec-pack root. It should not enable multi-source discovery from home config, `package.json`, or environment overlays in v1. The orchestrator benefits from one obvious config file and one obvious precedence model.\n\n### Validation Rules\n\n| Rule | Reason |\n|---|---|\n| `primary_harness` must be `claude-code` in v1 | v1 always runs inside Claude Code |\n| `secondary_harness` must be `codex`, `copilot`, or `none` | keep the contract explicit and narrow |\n| `none` means “use primary harness” | avoids extra indirection or derived config fields |\n| `story_implementor.secondary_harness !== \"copilot\"` in v1 | Copilot continuation is not supported for the retained implementor role |\n| `self_review.passes` must be `>= 1` and `<= 5` | prevents nonsensical or runaway values |\n| `epic_verifiers.length >= 1` | epic verification is mandatory |\n| duplicate epic verifier labels are invalid | makes downstream reporting legible |\n\n### Provider Availability Matrix\n\nThe CLI must validate not just schema shape, but whether the local machine can honor the requested harness assignments.\n\n```typescript\nexport interface HarnessAvailability {\n  harness: SecondaryHarness | PrimaryHarness;\n  available: boolean;\n  version?: string;\n  authStatus?: \"authenticated\" | \"unknown\" | \"missing\";\n  notes: string[];\n}\n\nexport interface ProviderMatrix {\n  primary: HarnessAvailability;\n  secondary: HarnessAvailability[];\n}\n```\n\nValidation behavior:\n\n- Always validate `claude-code` availability because it is the primary harness.\n- Validate `codex` only if at least one role requests `secondary_harness: \"codex\"`.\n- Validate `copilot` only if at least one role requests `secondary_harness: \"copilot\"`.\n- Skip provider-CLI checks for roles using `secondary_harness: \"none\"`.\n\n## Prompt Asset System\n\nPrompt assets are runtime architecture, not incidental text blobs. They should live in source form for maintainability, then be embedded into a generated TS module so the packaged runtime can ship as a single JS file.\n\n### Prompt Asset Layout\n\n```text\nprocesses/impl-cli/prompts/\n├── base/\n│   ├── story-implementor.md\n│   ├── story-verifier.md\n│   ├── quick-fixer.md\n│   ├── epic-verifier.md\n│   └── epic-synthesizer.md\n└── snippets/\n    ├── reading-journey.md\n    ├── report-contract.md\n    ├── gate-instructions.md\n    ├── mock-audit.md\n    ├── self-review-pass-1.md\n    ├── self-review-pass-2.md\n    └── self-review-pass-3.md\n```\n\n### Prompt Asset Content Contracts\n\nEach prompt asset should have a required internal contract, not just a filename.\n\n| Asset | Required Content |\n|---|---|\n| `story-implementor.md` | role stance, artifact reading order, implementation scope, self-review rules, JSON result contract |\n| `story-verifier.md` | evidence rules, severity enum, AC/TC coverage expectations, mock/shim audit rules, routing guidance |\n| `quick-fixer.md` | narrow-scope rule, forbidden full-story reimplementation rule, result contract |\n| `epic-verifier.md` | cross-story checks, architecture consistency, production-path mock/shim audit, verifier result contract |\n| `epic-synthesizer.md` | independent verification duty, confirmed vs disputed issue categories, synthesis result contract |\n| shared snippets | explicit placeholders, allowed runtime variables, and insertion order guarantees |\n\n### Prompt Assembly Pipeline\n\nPrompt assembly should be deterministic.\n\n1. Choose the role.\n2. Load that role’s base prompt.\n3. Append required reusable snippets for the operation.\n4. Append the relevant public insert file if present.\n5. Interpolate runtime values such as story path, tech-design shape, gate commands, and continuation handles.\n6. Emit one provider-ready prompt string and a compact assembly manifest for debugging.\n\n```typescript\nexport interface PromptAssemblyInput {\n  role:\n    | \"story_implementor\"\n    | \"story_verifier\"\n    | \"quick_fixer\"\n    | \"epic_verifier\"\n    | \"epic_synthesizer\";\n  storyId?: string;\n  storyPath?: string;\n  epicPath: string;\n  techDesignPath: string;\n  techDesignCompanionPaths: string[];\n  testPlanPath: string;\n  implementationPromptInsertPath?: string;\n  verifierPromptInsertPath?: string;\n  gateCommands: {\n    story?: string;\n    epic?: string;\n  };\n  selfReviewPass?: number;\n  followupRequest?: string;\n}\n\nexport interface PromptAssemblyResult {\n  prompt: string;\n  basePromptId: string;\n  snippetIds: string[];\n  publicInsertIds: string[];\n}\n```\n\nThe orchestrator does not need to see every runtime prompt asset, but the prompt-system reference should explain the mental model: base prompt plus snippets plus public insert plus runtime values.\n\nFor implementation, prompt assembly inputs should be modeled as discriminated unions by operation rather than as one giant loose object. The shared fields above describe the common shape; command-specific inputs should narrow them for:\n\n- story implement / continue\n- story verify\n- quick fix\n- epic cleanup\n- epic verify\n- epic synthesize\n\n## Flow 1: Inspect and Preflight\n\n**Covers:** AC-1.1, AC-1.2, AC-1.4, AC-1.5, AC-1.6, AC-2.1 to AC-2.4\n\nThis is the CLI’s setup boundary. `inspect` answers the structural question: is the spec pack arranged in a way the runtime understands? `preflight` answers the executable question: can this specific machine run the requested harness/model configuration with the required prompt assets and files in place?\n\nThis split matters. The orchestrator should be able to inspect a spec pack before it has chosen or authored a run config, but it should not launch any provider-backed work until preflight has passed.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Orchestrator\n    participant CLI\n    participant SpecPack\n    participant Config as impl-run.config.json\n    participant Providers as Provider CLIs\n\n    Note over Orchestrator,Providers: AC-1.1 to AC-2.4\n    Orchestrator->>CLI: inspect --spec-pack-root <path>\n    CLI->>SpecPack: Resolve required files, story order, insert presence\n    CLI-->>Orchestrator: Spec-pack summary\n    Orchestrator->>CLI: preflight --spec-pack-root <path>\n    CLI->>Config: Parse and validate run config\n    CLI->>Providers: Validate requested harness availability\n    CLI-->>Orchestrator: Readiness report + validated provider matrix\n```\n\n### Inspect Result Contract\n\n```typescript\nexport interface InspectResult {\n  status: \"ready\" | \"needs-user-decision\" | \"blocked\";\n  specPackRoot: string;\n  techDesignShape: \"two-file\" | \"four-file\";\n  artifacts: {\n    epicPath: string;\n    techDesignPath: string;\n    techDesignCompanionPaths: string[];\n    testPlanPath: string;\n    storiesDir: string;\n  };\n  stories: Array<{ id: string; title: string; path: string; order: number }>;\n  inserts: {\n    customStoryImplPromptInsert: \"present\" | \"absent\";\n    customStoryVerifierPromptInsert: \"present\" | \"absent\";\n  };\n  blockers: string[];\n  notes: string[];\n}\n```\n\n### Preflight Result Contract\n\n```typescript\nexport interface PreflightResult {\n  status: \"ready\" | \"needs-user-decision\" | \"blocked\";\n  validatedConfig: ImplRunConfig;\n  providerMatrix: ProviderMatrix;\n  verificationGates?: {\n    storyGate: string;\n    epicGate: string;\n    source: string;\n  };\n  configValidationNotes: string[];\n  promptAssets: {\n    basePromptsReady: boolean;\n    snippetsReady: boolean;\n    notes: string[];\n  };\n  blockers: string[];\n  notes: string[];\n}\n```\n\n`preflight` validates the authored config and reports the consequences of that config. It does **not** generate or rewrite the default matrix in v1. Default selection is part of the skill/process contract and is owned by the orchestrator before preflight runs.\n\n### Story Ordering and Story ID Rules\n\nSpec-pack resolution must not invent story order heuristically.\n\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

validated run config returned for log capture | `preflight-command.test.ts` | valid fixture | structured `validatedConfig` returned |\n\n## Flow 2: Story Implementation and Continuation\n\n**Covers:** AC-3.1, AC-3.2, AC-3.4a, AC-4.1 to AC-4.5\n\n`story-implement` launches the implementor for one story using the assembled prompt and the configured role assignment. The CLI owns prompt assembly, provider invocation, self-review prompt evolution, and the returned structured result. It does not own story acceptance. `story-continue` exists because the story implementor may keep working in the same provider session across follow-up fixes or post-review continuation.\n\nThe most important boundary here is continuity. The CLI may continue a provider session, but it must do so only from explicit continuation/session handles emitted in the previous result and handed back by the orchestrator. The CLI should never try to recover the “latest” session implicitly.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Orchestrator\n    participant CLI\n    participant Prompt as Prompt Assembly\n    participant Adapter as Provider Adapter\n    participant Harness as Secondary or Primary Harness\n\n    Note over Orchestrator,Harness: AC-3.1 to AC-4.5\n    Orchestrator->>CLI: story-implement --story-id <id>\n    CLI->>Prompt: Build implementor prompt\n    Prompt-->>CLI: assembled prompt + manifest\n    CLI->>Adapter: run implementor role\n    Adapter->>Harness: execute prompt\n    Harness-->>Adapter: result + session handle\n    CLI->>Prompt: run self-review pass 1..N\n    CLI-->>Orchestrator: structured implementor result + continuation handle\n```\n\n### Continuation Handle Contract\n\n```typescript\nexport interface ContinuationHandle {\n  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n  sessionId: string;\n  storyId: string;\n}\n```\n\n### Implementor Result Contract\n\n```typescript\nexport interface ImplementorResult {\n  resultId: string;\n  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n  model: string;\n  role: \"story_implementor\";\n  sessionId?: string;\n  outcome:\n    | \"ready-for-verification\"\n    | \"needs-followup-fix\"\n    | \"needs-human-ruling\"\n    | \"blocked\";\n  continuation?: ContinuationHandle;\n  story: {\n    id: string;\n    title: string;\n  };\n  planSummary: string;\n  changedFiles: Array<{ path: string; reason: string }>;\n  tests: {\n    added: string[];\n    modified: string[];\n    removed: string[];\n    totalAfterStory?: number;\n    deltaFromPriorBaseline?: number;\n  };\n  gatesRun: Array<{ command: string; result: \"pass\" | \"fail\" | \"not-run\" }>;\n  selfReview: {\n    passesRun: number;\n    findingsFixed: string[];\n    findingsSurfaced: string[];\n  };\n  openQuestions: string[];\n  specDeviations: string[];\n  recommendedNextStep: string;\n}\n```\n\n### Self-Review Rules\n\nThe CLI should own the mechanical shape of self-review:\n\n- Pass 1: broad correctness and obvious omissions\n- Pass 2: test/coverage and requirement alignment\n- Pass 3: residual risk and boundary audit\n\nStop rules:\n\n- stop early if the provider reports a blocking condition\n- stop early if a pass surfaces only human-ruling issues\n- otherwise run the configured number of passes\n\n### TC Mapping for This Flow\n\n| TC | Tests | Module | Setup | Assert |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| TC-3.1a | implementor prompt assembly uses base + snippets | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | They should not reuse prior verifier sessions. `quick-fix` is intentionally lighter than a full implementor pass. It exists for small, bounded corrections that do not justify re-running the full story implementation workflow.\n\nThe orchestration rule here is simple. The CLI can execute verification or a quick fix, but it cannot decide that a story is accepted. It only returns disciplined findings and outcomes. The orchestrator still interprets them and runs the final gate.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Orchestrator\n    participant CLI\n    participant Adapter1 as Verifier Adapter A\n    participant Adapter2 as Verifier Adapter B\n    participant QuickFix as Quick Fix Adapter\n\n    Note over Orchestrator,QuickFix: AC-5.1 to AC-5.5\n    Orchestrator->>CLI: story-verify --story-id <id>\n    CLI->>Adapter1: launch verifier 1\n    CLI->>Adapter2: launch verifier 2\n    Adapter1-->>CLI: verifier result\n    Adapter2-->>CLI: verifier result\n    CLI-->>Orchestrator: verifier batch\n    Orchestrator->>CLI: quick-fix --request ...\n    CLI->>QuickFix: run bounded correction\n    QuickFix-->>CLI: quick-fix result\n    CLI-->>Orchestrator: quick-fix result\n```\n\n### Story Verifier Result Contract\n\n```typescript\nexport interface StoryVerifierResult {\n  resultId: string;\n  outcome: \"pass\" | \"revise\" | \"block\";\n  verifierLabel: string;\n  provider: VerifierFinding[];\n  requirementCoverage: {\n    verified: string[];\n    unverified: string[];\n  };\n  gatesRun: Array<{ command: string; result: \"pass\" | \"fail\" | \"not-run\" }>;\n  mockOrShimAuditFindings: string[];\n  recommendedFixScope:\n    | \"same-session-implementor\"\n    | \"quick-fix\"\n    | \"fresh-fix-path\"\n    | \"human-ruling\";\n  openQuestions: string[];\n  additionalObservations: string[];\n}\n```\n\n```typescript\nexport type FindingSeverity = \"critical\" | \"major\" | \"minor\" | \"observation\";\nexport type RequirementId = string;\n\nexport interface VerifierFinding {\n  id: string;\n  severity: FindingSeverity;\n  title: string;\n  evidence: string;\n  affectedFiles: string[];\n  requirementIds: RequirementId[];\n  recommendedFixScope:\n    | \"same-session-implementor\"\n    | \"quick-fix\"\n    | \"fresh-fix-path\"\n    | \"human-ruling\";\n  blocking: boolean;\n}\n```\n\n### Quick Fix Result Contract\n\n```typescript\nexport interface QuickFixResult {\n  resultId: string;\n  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n  model: string;\n  outcome:\n    | \"ready-for-verification\"\n    | \"needs-more-routing\"\n    | \"blocked\";\n  filesChanged: string[];\n  changeSummary: string;\n  gatesRun: Array<{ command: string; result: \"pass\" | \"fail\" | \"not-run\" }>;\n  unresolvedConcerns: string[];\n  recommendedNextStep: string;\n}\n```\n\n### TC Mapping for This Flow\n\n| TC | Tests | Module | Setup | Assert |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| TC-3.3a | `cli-operations-doc.test.ts` | command guide fixture | public docs state gate is orchestrator-owned |\n\n## Flow 4: Epic Cleanup, Verification, and Synthesis\n\n**Covers:** AC-7.1 to AC-7.3, AC-8.1 to AC-8.4\n\nAt epic closeout the CLI executes three different kinds of work: cleanup-only correction, epic-level verifier batches, and synthesis. The orchestrator still determines whether the run can close. The CLI should never blur these responsibilities into one vague “finish the epic” command.\n\nThe cleanup operation is distinct because it acts on an orchestrator-curated cleanup batch. It is not a general-purpose retry. It is a bounded correction-only workflow designed to start epic verification from the cleaned state rather than from an accumulation of known-but-deferred issues.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Orchestrator\n    participant CLI\n    participant Cleanup as Cleanup Runner\n    participant EV as Epic Verifier Batch\n    participant SYN as Epic Synthesizer\n\n    Note over Orchestrator,SYN: AC-7.1 to AC-8.4\n    Orchestrator->>CLI: epic-cleanup --batch <cleanup.md>\n    CLI->>Cleanup: apply approved cleanup items\n    Cleanup-->>CLI: cleanup result\n    CLI-->>Orchestrator: cleanup outcome\n    Orchestrator->>CLI: epic-verify\n    CLI->>EV: launch epic verifiers\n    EV-->>CLI: epic verifier results\n    CLI-->>Orchestrator: verifier batch\n    Orchestrator->>CLI: epic-synthesize\n    CLI->>SYN: verify and consolidate findings\n    SYN-->>CLI: synthesis result\n    CLI-->>Orchestrator: synthesis outcome\n```\n\n### Epic Verifier Result\n\n```typescript\nexport interface EpicVerifierResult {\n  resultId: string;\n  outcome: \"pass\" | \"revise\" | \"block\";\n  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n  model: string;\n  reviewerLabel: string;\n  crossStoryFindings: string[];\n  architectureFindings: string[];\n  epicCoverageAssessment: string[];\n  mockOrShimAuditFindings: string[];\n  blockingFindings: VerifierFinding[];\n  nonBlockingFindings: VerifierFinding[];\n  unresolvedItems: string[];\n  gateResult: \"pass\" | \"fail\" | \"not-run\";\n}\n```\n\n### Epic Cleanup Result\n\n```typescript\nexport interface EpicCleanupResult {\n  resultId: string;\n  outcome: \"cleaned\" | \"needs-more-cleanup\" | \"blocked\";\n  cleanupBatchPath: string;\n  filesChanged: string[];\n  changeSummary: string;\n  gatesRun: Array<{ command: string; result: \"pass\" | \"fail\" | \"not-run\" }>;\n  unresolvedConcerns: string[];\n  recommendedNextStep: string;\n}\n```\n\n### Epic Synthesis Result\n\n```typescript\nexport interface EpicSynthesisResult {\n  resultId: string;\n  outcome:\n    | \"ready-for-closeout\"\n    | \"needs-fixes\"\n    | \"needs-more-verification\"\n    | \"blocked\";\n  confirmedIssues: string[];\n  disputedOrUnconfirmedIssues: string[];\n  readinessAssessment: string;\n  recommendedNextStep: string;\n}\n```\n\n### TC Mapping for This Flow\n\n| TC | Tests | Module | Setup | Assert |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| TC-7.1a | cleanup result contract exists | `epic-cleanup-command.test.ts` | cleanup batch fixture | verification begins |\n| TC-8.1a | epic verification mandatory | `cli-operations-doc.test.ts` | closeout docs fixture | no closeout path without `epic-verify` |\n| TC-8.1b | no skip path | result |\n| TC-8.3a | synthesis verifies rather than merges blindly | `epic-synthesize-command.test.ts` | conflicting verifier reports | synthesis distinguishes confirmed vs unconfirmed issues |\n| TC-8.4a | final epic gate remains orchestrator-owned | `cli-operations-doc.test.ts` | command guide fixture | docs state final gate is outside CLI |\n\n## Provider Adapters\n\nThe provider adapters let the CLI execute the same operation contract against different harnesses without changing the orchestrator-facing command surface.\n\n```typescript\nexport interface ProviderExecutionRequest {\n  prompt: string;\n  cwd: string;\n  model: string;\n  reasoningEffort: string;\n  resumeSessionId?: string;\n  timeoutMs: number;\n}\n\nexport interface ProviderExecutionResult {\n  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n  sessionId?: string;\n  stdout: string;\n  stderr: string;\n  exitCode: number;\n  parsedResult?: unknown;\n}\n\nexport interface ProviderAdapter {\n  checkAvailability(): Promise<HarnessAvailability>;\n  execute(request: ProviderExecutionRequest): Promise<ProviderExecutionResult>;\n}\n```\n\nAdapter rules:\n\n- `claude-code` adapter uses the primary harness path and supports “no secondary harness” roles.\n- `codex` adapter supports runtime model and reasoning-effort overrides.\n- `copilot` adapter supports runtime model override, but reasoning effort is effectively config-level and should be treated as advisory in validation notes.\n- Adapters must return explicit session IDs when the provider exposes them. The CLI should never rely on “latest session for cwd” behavior.\n\n### Provider Invocation Contracts\n\nThe adapter behavior must be explicit per harness.\n\n| Harness | Invocation Shape | Resume Shape | Notes |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `codex` | `codex exec --json -m <model> -c model_reasoning_effort=<effort> \"<prompt>\"` | `codex exec resume --json <session-id> \"<follow-up prompt>\"` | use explicit session IDs only; capture stdout to artifact before parsing |\n| `copilot` | `copilot -p \"<prompt>\" -s --model <model> ...` with adapter-owned result capture and permission flags | no session resume contract for retained implementor in v1; use fresh programmatic runs only | Copilot is valid for fresh-session roles only in v1; reasoning effort treated as advisory because it is config-level |\n| `claude-code` | `claude -p \"<prompt>\" --output-format json --model <model> --permission-mode <mode>` | `claude -p \"<follow-up prompt>\" --resume <session-id> --output-format json --model <model>` | do not reuse the live interactive orchestrator session; do not rely on `--continue` or latest-session-by-cwd behavior |\n\nThe v1 design assumes the primary harness is still invokable as a child process. If that proves false in implementation, the design must be updated explicitly rather than allowing the runtime model to drift into “emit a prompt and ask the orchestrator to run it manually.”\n\n### `story-continue` Resolution Rules\n\n`story-continue` resolves `model` and `reasoningEffort` from the active `story_implementor` entry in the selected config file unless a future continuation-handle schema explicitly carries immutable model metadata. The continuation handle is intentionally light in v1 and should not become a second config surface.\n\n### Availability and Auth Checks\n\n| Harness | Version Check | Auth Check | Availability Tier Notes |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `claude-code` | `claude --version` | `claude auth status` | supports `binary-present` and `authenticated-known` tiers |\n| `codex` | `codex --version` | explicit auth-state command if available, otherwise mark auth as `unknown` without paid smoke by default | supports `binary-present`; `authenticated-known` only if a non-mutating auth check exists |\n| `copilot` | `copilot --version` | explicit auth-state command if available, otherwise mark auth as `unknown` without paid smoke by default | `--npx` fallback deferred in v1 |\n\nAvailability tiers:\n\n- `binary-present`\n- `authenticated-known`\n- `auth-unknown`\n- `unavailable`\n\n### Provider Output Parsing Rules\n\n- Provider stdout is captured in full to a temp/result artifact before parsing.\n- The adapter validates the parsed provider result before converting it into the command result envelope.\n- Invalid structured output becomes a machine-readable CLI error with a stable error code.\n- Missing session IDs are treated as blocking for commands that require continuation support.\n- Provider stderr may be persisted for debugging, but it is not the orchestration source of truth.\n\nFor v1, provider prompts require the model to return exactly one JSON object matching the operation payload schema. The adapter accepts only:\n\n1. provider-native JSON whose final text/result field is exactly that object, or\n2. raw stdout that is exactly that object.\n\nThe adapter does not parse arbitrary prose, markdown summaries, or fenced JSON blocks in v1. Invalid output returns `PROVIDER_OUTPUT_INVALID`.\n\n## Verification Gate Resolution\n\nThe runtime must not guess gate commands ad hoc.\n\n1. explicit command flags\n2. explicit run-config values, if later added\n3. discovered package scripts\n4. supporting policy docs or CI config\n5. otherwise `needs-user-decision`\n\n```typescript\nexport interface VerificationGateConfig {\n  storyGate: string;\n  epicGate: string;\n  source: \"cli-flag\" | \"run-config\" | \"package-scripts\" | \"policy-doc\" | \"ci-config\";\n}\n```\n\nThe preflight result should surface this config so the orchestrator can persist it into `team-impl-log.md`.\n\n### Canonical Gate Mapping\n\nUntil a project overrides the defaults, the expected mapping for this repo is:\n\n| Gate | Command |\n|---|---|\n| Story gate | `bun run green-verify` |\n| Epic gate | `bun run verify-all` |\n\nThis mapping should appear consistently in:\n\n- `preflight` output\n- `team-impl-log.md`\n- prompt assembly inputs\n- process-playbook examples\n\n## Packaging and Build Integration\n\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/artifacts/story-08/001-implementor.jsonl:82:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_44","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' src/references/claude-impl-cli-operations.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Claude Impl CLI Operations\n\nThis guide explains the public CLI from the orchestrator's perspective. The CLI handles one bounded operation at a time and returns a structured envelope that the orchestrator records in `team-impl-log.md`.\n\n## Command Map\n\n| Command | Use It When | Outcome States | What To Do Next |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `inspect` | You need to confirm the spec-pack layout, tech-design shape, story inventory, and insert presence. | `ready`, `needs-user-decision`, `blocked` | Record the layout and continue to the reading journey or pause for a user decision. |\n| `preflight` | The reading journey is complete and `impl-run.config.json` exists. | `ready`, `needs-user-decision`, `blocked` | Record the validated configuration and gate choices before story work. |\n| `story-implement` | A story is ready to start. | `ready-for-verification`, `needs-followup-fix`, `needs-human-ruling`, `blocked` | Route verification or follow-up work based on the returned outcome. |\n| `story-continue` | The active retained implementor session should continue. | `ready-for-verification`, `needs-followup-fix`, `needs-human-ruling`, `blocked` | Continue the same story session only when the continuation handle matches. |\n| `story-verify` | Fresh verifiers should review one story. | `pass`, `revise`, `block` | Route fixes or move toward acceptance. |\n| `quick-fix` | A small, bounded fix should run without restarting the full implementor workflow. | `ready-for-verification`, `needs-more-routing`, `blocked` | Re-verify or route a larger follow-up path. |\n| `epic-cleanup` | Approved cleanup-only fixes should run before epic verification. | `cleaned`, `needs-more-cleanup`, `blocked` | Review the cleanup result before epic verification continues. |\n| `epic-verify` | All stories are accepted and cleanup is complete. | `pass`, `revise`, `block` | Gather the verifier batch for synthesis. |\n| `epic-synthesize` | Epic verifier results are available and need consolidation. | `ready-for-closeout`, `needs-fixes`, `needs-more-verification`, `blocked` | Run the final orchestrator-owned gate or route more work. |\n\n## Routing Signals\n\n- If `story-implement` returns `needs-human-ruling`, keep the surfaced uncertainty explicit and pause for an orchestrator or human routing decision.\n- If verifier results disagree materially, keep both verifier results visible, route to fresh follow-up verification or human escalation, and do not pretend the disagreement is already resolved.\n- Use `quick-fix` only for small mechanical corrections. Pass a plain-language task description and do not impose a story-aware structured result contract on that handoff.\n\n## IO Contract\n\n- With `--json`, stdout must contain exactly one JSON envelope.\n- The same envelope is persisted under `<spec-pack-root>/artifacts/`.\n- `stderr` is for progress or debugging only; it is not the routing source of truth.\n\n## Ownership Boundary\n\nThe final story gate and final epic gate remain orchestrator-owned. The CLI can report readiness, verification findings, and cleanup outcomes, but it does not accept stories or close the epic by itself.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/artifacts/story-08/001-implementor.jsonl:84:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_45","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,320p' src/references/claude-impl-process-playbook.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Claude Impl Process Playbook\n\nThis is the main process reference after setup. Use it to decide which bounded CLI operation comes next and what evidence must be written back into `team-impl-log.md`.\n\n## Stage Map\n\n1. Setup: resolve the spec pack, initialize or resume `team-impl-log.md`, and discover verification gates.\n2. Preflight: validate the authored run configuration before any provider-backed work starts.\n3. Story cycle: implement, verify, run the final story gate, record the story receipt, then decide whether to advance.\n4. Closeout: complete cleanup, epic verification, synthesis, and the final orchestrator-owned gate.\n\n## Setup Rules\n\n- Use `inspect` for structural discovery.\n- Do not start implementation until the story gate and epic gate are recorded.\n- If gate policy is ambiguous after checking project policy docs and package scripts, pause with a user decision instead of guessing.\n- Resume from disk artifacts, not conversation memory.\n\n## Durable Artifacts\n\n- `impl-run.config.json`: the run shape chosen by the orchestrator\n- `team-impl-log.md`: the durable record of state, gates, receipts, and risks\n- CLI result artifacts under `artifacts/`: machine-readable evidence returned by each bounded operation\n\n## Story Receipt Minimum\n\nBefore accepting a story, record:\n\n- the story id and title\n- the implementor result artifact\n- the verifier result artifacts\n- the exact story gate command and result\n- the disposition of every unresolved finding\n- Use `fixed`, `accepted-risk`, and `defer` as the allowed disposition labels.\n- the open risks that remain after the acceptance decision\n- the cumulative baseline before and after the story\n\n## Story Acceptance Gate\n\n- When implementation evidence and verifier results are complete, run the final story gate yourself instead of treating verifier output as acceptance.\n- Run `bun run green-verify` as the canonical story gate unless the recorded project policy says otherwise.\n- Do not accept the story until the recorded gate result, finding dispositions, and open-risk review all support progression.\n\n## Story Progression Rules\n\n- Prepare the next story from the committed codebase, the ordered story list, `team-impl-log.md`, and the recorded artifacts on disk.\n- Do not rely on ad hoc memory or prior chat context to decide what happens next.\n- Compare the current total test count to the prior accepted baseline before accepting the story.\n- If the current total is lower than the prior accepted baseline, treat it as a regression and block acceptance until resolved.\n- If the current total meets or exceeds the prior accepted baseline, record the new baseline and continue the acceptance decision.\n- After acceptance, update the receipt and cumulative baseline fields before moving the log back to `BETWEEN_STORIES`.\n- Advance to the next story only after acceptance is recorded, the receipt is complete, the cumulative baseline is updated, and the log state returns to `BETWEEN_STORIES`.\n\n## Fix Routing Rules\n\n- If `story-implement` returns `needs-human-ruling`, keep the surfaced uncertainty explicit and pause for an orchestrator routing decision instead of auto-fixing it away.\n- When that uncertainty is surfaced, choose explicitly from this routing menu:\n  - `story-continue` with the same-session implementor when more context-aware follow-up work can likely resolve it without changing the plan shape.\n  - `quick-fix` for a small bounded correction that does not justify restarting the full story implementor workflow.\n  - Human escalation when design ambiguity or product intent is still blocking progress.\n- If verifier results disagree materially, do not auto-resolve the disagreement. Keep both verifier result sets visible and route to fresh follow-up verification or human escalation.\n- Use `quick-fix` only for small mechanical corrections that do not justify restarting the full story implementor workflow.\n\n## Recovery Rule\n\nIf execution is interrupted, restart from the files on disk: `team-impl-log.md`, `impl-run.config.json`, and the command result artifacts already written under `artifacts/`.\n\n- Recover the active story, current phase, and any retained implementor continuation handle from `team-impl-log.md` before dispatching more work.\n- Keep `Current Phase` coarse and `Last Completed Checkpoint` specific so an interrupted story cycle can resume from the last durable step.\n- Compare the recorded phase and checkpoint to the expected result artifact for that phase before resuming.\n- If an in-flight implementor, quick-fix, or verifier step has no durable result artifact yet, treat it as incomplete rather than assuming it finished.\n- Use the recorded artifact paths to reopen the latest implementor and verifier evidence instead of reconstructing state from memory.\n- Use completed artifacts already on disk as evidence and route only the missing or invalidated work.\n- Replay from the last completed checkpoint when the durable inputs on disk make that replay safe.\n- If the replay boundary is unclear, pause for human ruling instead of guessing from memory.\n- Do not ask for the prior conversation to be restored before resuming the run.\n- Resume from the durable files on disk even when the orchestration session has been compacted or restarted.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/artifacts/story-08/001-implementor.jsonl:92:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_49","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' src/phases/claude-impl.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Claude Implementation\n\nUse `ls-claude-impl` when a completed spec pack already exists and Claude Code needs a disciplined implementation workflow with a bundled bounded-operation CLI.\n\n## Startup Orientation\n\nResolve the spec-pack root before reading deeply. The minimum v1 layout is:\n\n- `epic.md`\n- `tech-design.md`\n- `test-plan.md`\n- `stories/`\n\nRun `bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs inspect --spec-pack-root <path> --json` as the first runtime check. Capture the returned tech-design shape, required artifacts, story inventory, and insert status before you move on to run configuration or provider-backed work.\n\nRead every story file in `stories/` in order before starting Story 0 or Story 1. Do not begin implementation from a partial story read.\n\n## Initialization Steps\n\n1. Confirm the spec-pack root and run `inspect`.\n2. If `team-impl-log.md` is absent, create `team-impl-log.md` and initialize the run in `SETUP`.\n3. If `team-impl-log.md` already exists, read it first and resume from the recorded state.\n4. Treat `team-impl-log.md`, `impl-run.config.json`, and the CLI result artifacts under `artifacts/` as the recovery surface for a resumed run.\n5. Treat missing prior chat or tool-call context as a normal recovery case, not a blocker.\n6. Record whether the spec pack is a two-file or four-file tech-design layout.\n7. Record whether `custom-story-impl-prompt-insert.md` and `custom-story-verifier-prompt-insert.md` are present. Their absence is non-blocking.\n8. Discover verification gates in this precedence order: explicit CLI flags, `impl-run.config.json` entries if the run uses them later, package scripts, project policy docs, then CI configuration.\n9. If the policy stays ambiguous after that precedence order, pause for a user decision instead of improvising.\n10. Run `preflight` before provider-backed work.\n11. After `preflight`, record the validated run configuration, available providers, active role defaults, verification gates, and degraded fallback conditions in `team-impl-log.md`.\n\n## Default Resolution Contract\n\n- Claude Code is the primary harness for every run.\n- If Codex CLI is available, default the story implementor to Codex `gpt-5.4` with high reasoning.\n- If Codex CLI is available, default the verifier pair to Codex `gpt-5.4` with extra high reasoning plus Claude Sonnet with high reasoning.\n- If Codex CLI is available, default epic verifier GPT lanes and the epic synthesizer to Codex `gpt-5.4` with extra high reasoning, paired with a Claude Sonnet verifier lane.\n- If Codex is unavailable but Copilot CLI is available, use Copilot for the fresh-session GPT roles and keep the retained story implementor on Claude Sonnet high.\n- If no GPT-capable secondary harness is available, switch the run to Claude-only defaults, record the degraded-diversity condition explicitly, and note that verifier diversity is weaker in this run.\n\n## State Ownership\n\n- `impl-run.config.json` is orchestrator-owned configuration.\n- `team-impl-log.md` is the durable run record.\n- The CLI performs one bounded operation per call and returns structured results.\n- The CLI does not own story progression, acceptance, or recovery strategy between calls.\n- Run the final story gate yourself after implementation evidence and verifier results are complete.\n- Compare the current total test count to the prior accepted baseline before accepting a story.\n- Advance to the next story only from the committed codebase, the ordered story list, `team-impl-log.md`, and the recorded artifacts on disk.\n\n## Read Next\n\nAfter the initial `inspect` pass and log setup, read:\n\n1. `references/claude-impl-reading-journey.md`\n2. `references/claude-impl-process-playbook.md`\n3. `references/claude-impl-cli-operations.md`\n4. `references/claude-impl-prompt-system.md`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/artifacts/story-05/003-implementor-self-review-2.jsonl:5:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' src/references/claude-impl-process-playbook.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Claude Impl Process Playbook\n\nThis is the main process reference after setup. Use it to decide which bounded CLI operation comes next and what evidence must be written back into `team-impl-log.md`.\n\n## Stage Map\n\n1. Setup: resolve the spec pack, initialize or resume `team-impl-log.md`, and discover verification gates.\n2. Preflight: validate the authored run configuration before any provider-backed work starts.\n3. Story cycle: implement, verify, route fixes, then record the story receipt.\n4. Closeout: complete cleanup, epic verification, synthesis, and the final orchestrator-owned gate.\n\n## Setup Rules\n\n- Use `inspect` for structural discovery.\n- Do not start implementation until the story gate and epic gate are recorded.\n- If gate policy is ambiguous after checking project policy docs and package scripts, pause with a user decision instead of guessing.\n- Resume from disk artifacts, not conversation memory.\n\n## Durable Artifacts\n\n- `impl-run.config.json`: the run shape chosen by the orchestrator\n- `team-impl-log.md`: the durable record of state, gates, receipts, and risks\n- CLI result artifacts under `artifacts/`: machine-readable evidence returned by each bounded operation\n\n## Story Receipt Minimum\n\nBefore accepting a story, record:\n\n- the story id and title\n- the command result artifacts that justify progression\n- the exact story gate command and result\n- the disposition of every unresolved finding\n- the cumulative baseline before and after the story\n\n## Recovery Rule\n\nIf execution is interrupted, restart from the files on disk: `team-impl-log.md`, `impl-run.config.json`, and the command result artifacts already written under `artifacts/`.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/artifacts/story-05/003-implementor-self-review-2.jsonl:7:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_2","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' processes/impl-cli/tests/quick-fix-command.test.ts\"","aggregated_output":"import { join } from \"node:path\";\nimport { expect, test } from \"bun:test\";\n\nimport {\n  createRunConfig,\n  createSpecPack,\n  createTempDir,\n  parseJsonOutput,\n  readJsonLines,\n  runSourceCli,\n  writeFakeProviderExecutable,\n  writeRunConfig,\n  writeTextFile,\n} from \"./test-helpers\";\n\nasync function createQuickFixSpecPack(scope: string): Promise<string> {\n  const specPackRoot = await createSpecPack(scope, {\n    companionMode: \"four-file\",\n  });\n  await writeTextFile(\n    join(specPackRoot, \"package.json\"),\n    `${JSON.stringify(\n      {\n        name: \"fixture-spec-pack\",\n        private: true,\n        scripts: {\n          \"green-verify\": \"bun run green-verify\",\n          \"verify-all\": \"bun run verify-all\",\n        },\n      },\n      null,\n      2\n    )}\\n`\n  );\n\n  return specPackRoot;\n}\n\ntest(\"TC-5.3a runs quick-fix from request-text without requiring story-aware inputs or restarting the full implementor path\", async () => {\n  const specPackRoot = await createQuickFixSpecPack(\"quick-fix-request-text\");\n  await writeRunConfig(specPackRoot, createRunConfig());\n  const providerBinDir = await createTempDir(\"quick-fix-request-text-provider\");\n  const requestText =\n    \"Fix the failing quick-fix contract assertions only. Do not widen scope.\";\n  const rawProviderOutput = [\n    \"{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"message\\\":\\\"Applying the requested correction.\\\"}\",\n    \"{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"message\\\":\\\"Done.\\\"}\",\n  ].join(\"\\n\");\n  const { env, logPath } = await writeFakeProviderExecutable({\n    binDir: providerBinDir,\n    provider: \"codex\",\n    responses: [\n      {\n        stdout: rawProviderOutput,\n      },\n    ],\n  });\n\n  const run = await runSourceCli(\n    [\n      \"quick-fix\",\n      \"--spec-pack-root\",\n      specPackRoot,\n      \"--request-text\",\n      requestText,\n      \"--json\",\n    ],\n    {\n      env: {\n        PATH: `${providerBinDir}:${process.env.PATH ?? \"\"}`,\n        ...env,\n      },\n    }\n  );\n\n  expect(run.exitCode).toBe(0);\n\n  const envelope = parseJsonOutput<any>(run.stdout);\n  expect(envelope.command).toBe(\"quick-fix\");\n  expect(envelope.status).toBe(\"ok\");\n  expect(envelope.outcome).toBe(\"ready-for-verification\");\n  expect(envelope.result).toMatchObject({\n    provider: \"codex\",\n    model: \"gpt-5.4\",\n    rawProviderOutput,\n  });\n\n  const artifactPath = envelope.artifacts[0].path as string;\n  expect(artifactPath).toContain(\"/artifacts/quick-fix/001-quick-fix.json\");\n  const persisted = JSON.parse(await Bun.file(artifactPath).text());\n  expect(persisted).toEqual(envelope);\n\n  const invocations = await readJsonLines<{ args: string[] }>(logPath);\n  expect(invocations).toHaveLength(1);\n  expect(invocations[0]?.args).toEqual([\n    \"exec\",\n    \"--json\",\n    \"-m\",\n    \"gpt-5.4\",\n    \"-c\",\n    \"model_reasoning_effort=medium\",\n    requestText,\n  ]);\n  expect(invocations[0]?.args).not.toContain(\"resume\");\n  expect(invocations[0]?.args[invocations[0].args.length - 1]).toBe(requestText);\n});\n\ntest(\"TC-5.3b accepts --request-file, uses the selected working directory, and keeps provider output free-form inside the envelope\", async () => {\n  const specPackRoot = await createQuickFixSpecPack(\"quick-fix-request-file\");\n  await writeRunConfig(specPackRoot, createRunConfig());\n  const providerBinDir = await createTempDir(\"quick-fix-request-file-provider\");\n  const rawProviderOutput =\n    \"Applied the requested fix and left one note for the orchestrator to review.\";\n  const { env, logPath } = await writeFakeProviderExecutable({\n    binDir: providerBinDir,\n    provider: \"codex\",\n    responses: [\n      {\n        stdout: rawProviderOutput,\n      },\n    ],\n  });\n  const requestFilePath = join(specPackRoot, \"quick-fix-request.txt\");\n  const requestText = [\n    \"Update the quick-fix docs to reflect the plain-language handoff.\",\n    \"Keep the change bounded to the Story 5 quick-fix surface.\",\n  ].join(\"\\n\");\n  await writeTextFile(requestFilePath, requestText);\n  const workingDirectory = await createTempDir(\"quick-fix-working-directory\");\n\n  const run = await runSourceCli(\n    [\n      \"quick-fix\",\n      \"--spec-pack-root\",\n      specPackRoot,\n      \"--request-file\",\n      requestFilePath,\n      \"--working-directory\",\n      workingDirectory,\n      \"--json\",\n    ],\n    {\n      env: {\n        PATH: `${providerBinDir}:${process.env.PATH ?? \"\"}`,\n        ...env,\n      },\n    }\n  );\n\n  expect(run.exitCode).toBe(0);\n\n  const envelope = parseJsonOutput<any>(run.stdout);\n  expect(envelope.result).toMatchObject({\n    provider: \"codex\",\n    model: \"gpt-5.4\",\n    rawProviderOutput,\n  });\n  expect(envelope.result.filesChanged).toBeUndefined();\n  expect(envelope.result.changeSummary).toBeUndefined();\n  expect(envelope.result.unresolvedConcerns).toBeUndefined();\n\n  const invocations = await readJsonLines<{ args: string[]; cwd: string }>(logPath);\n  expect(invocations).toHaveLength(1);\n  expect(invocations[0]?.cwd).toBe(workingDirectory);\n  expect(invocations[0]?.args[invocations[0].args.length - 1]).toBe(requestText);\n});\n\ntest(\"routes quick-fix to needs-more-routing when the provider returns no stdout to route from\", async () => {\n  const specPackRoot = await createQuickFixSpecPack(\"quick-fix-empty-output\");\n  await writeRunConfig(specPackRoot, createRunConfig());\n  const providerBinDir = await createTempDir(\"quick-fix-empty-output-provider\");\n  const { env } = await writeFakeProviderExecutable({\n    binDir: providerBinDir,\n    provider: \"codex\",\n    responses: [\n      {\n        stdout: \"\",\n      },\n    ],\n  });\n\n  const run = await runSourceCli(\n    [\n      \"quick-fix\",\n      \"--spec-pack-root\",\n      specPackRoot,\n      \"--request-text\",\n      \"Apply the tiny routing correction and report back.\",\n      \"--json\",\n    ],\n    {\n      env: {\n        PATH: `${providerBinDir}:${process.env.PATH ?? \"\"}`,\n        ...env,\n      },\n    }\n  );\n\n  expect(run.exitCode).toBe(2);\n\n  const envelope = parseJsonOutput<any>(run.stdout);\n  expect(envelope.status).toBe(\"ok\");\n  expect(envelope.outcome).toBe(\"needs-more-routing\");\n  expect(envelope.result.rawProviderOutput).toBe(\"\");\n});\n\ntest(\"rejects legacy story-aware flags such as --story-id instead of silently accepting them\", async () => {\n  const specPackRoot = await createQuickFixSpecPack(\"quick-fix-reject-story-id\");\n  await writeRunConfig(specPackRoot, createRunConfig());\n\n  const run = await runSourceCli([\n    \"quick-fix\",\n    \"--spec-pack-root\",\n    specPackRoot,\n    \"--request-text\",\n    \"Apply the narrow correction only.\",\n    \"--story-id\",\n    \"story-05\",\n    \"--json\",\n  ]);\n\n  expect(run.exitCode).toBe(1);\n\n  const envelope = parseJsonOutput<any>(run.stdout);\n  expect(envelope.status).toBe(\"error\");\n  expect(envelope.errors).toEqual(\n    expect.arrayContaining([\n      expect.objectContaining({\n        code: \"INVALID_INVOCATION\",\n        message: \"quick-fix does not accept story-aware flags such as --story-id or --scope-file.\",\n      }),\n    ])\n  );\n});\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/artifacts/story-03/005-verifier-codex54-xhigh.jsonl:12:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/stories/03-story-implementor-workflow.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Story 3: Story Implementor Workflow\n\n### Summary\n<!-- Jira: Summary field -->\nLaunch the retained story implementor, keep it in the same session across self-review, and return a structured implementor result with continuation details.\n\n### Description\n<!-- Jira: Description field -->\n\n**User Profile**\n\n- **Primary User:** A Claude Code orchestration agent operating on behalf of a senior engineer or tech lead\n- **Context:** Running a completed spec pack through a disciplined implementation workflow without relying on conversation memory or ad hoc orchestration\n- **Mental Model:** \"I load a finished spec pack, confirm the environment and options, launch the right workflow through the CLI, and supervise implementation and verification until the epic is complete.\"\n- **Key Constraint:** Claude Code is always available. GPT model lanes depend on whether Codex CLI or Copilot CLI is available in the local environment.\n\n**Objective**\n\nExecute one story implementation call through the CLI, keep the implementor in a retained session for that story, run evolving self-review passes inside the same session, apply only non-controversial fixes automatically, and return a structured result that the orchestrator can route and persist.\n\n**In Scope**\n\n- Story implementor launch through the CLI\n- Retained-session continuity for implementor + self-review\n- Three-pass self-review with evolving prompts\n- Automatic application of non-controversial fixes only\n- Structured implementor result with continuation/session fields\n\n**Out of Scope**\n\n- Fresh verifier batch execution\n- Quick-fix workflow\n- Story acceptance and progression\n- Resume from interruption after the orchestrator session is lost\n\n**Dependencies**\n\n- Story 2 complete\n- Prompt assembly and reading-journey contracts available\n- Run config resolved and provider availability confirmed\n\n### Acceptance Criteria\n<!-- Jira: Acceptance Criteria field -->\n\n**AC-4.1:** Story implementation is launched through a documented CLI contract\n\n- **TC-4.1a: Implementor launch**\n  - Given: The orchestrator is ready to start a story\n  - When: It calls the story implementor CLI operation\n  - Then: The CLI accepts the documented inputs, reads the specified spec-pack artifacts, and returns a structured implementation result\n\n**AC-4.2:** The story implementor remains a retained session for the duration of the story workflow unless replaced\n\n- **TC-4.2a: Same-session continuity**\n  - Given: Story implementation completes successfully\n  - When: Self-review begins\n  - Then: Self-review runs in the same implementor session rather than a fresh session\n- **TC-4.2b: Session continuation fields returned**\n  - Given: The CLI finishes a story implementation call\n  - When: It returns the structured result\n  - Then: The result includes the provider identifier and session identifier required to continue that implementor session later\n\n**AC-4.3:** Default self-review uses three passes with evolving prompts\n\n- **TC-4.3a: Three self-review passes**\n  - Given: A story implementation call reaches self-review\n  - When: The CLI runs the default self-review cycle\n  - Then: It runs three self-review passes unless the workflow stops early because of a blocking condition\n- **TC-4.3b: Evolving self-review prompt**\n  - Given: The CLI is running multiple self-review passes\n  - When: It advances from one pass to the next\n  - Then: Each pass adjusts the self-review prompt so the implementor does not repeat the same review request unchanged\n\n**AC-4.4:** Self-review applies only non-controversial fixes automatically\n\n- **TC-4.4a: Non-controversial fix**\n  - Given: A self-review pass finds a clear and local correction with no meaningful design ambiguity\n  - When: The implementor can fix it confidently\n  - Then: The CLI allows that fix to be applied during the same story implementation call\n- **TC-4.4b: Uncertain fix**\n  - Given: A self-review pass finds a potential issue but the implementor is not confident the change is the right fix\n  - When: The self-review cycle completes\n  - Then: The issue is surfaced to the orchestrator for routing instead of being fixed automatically\n\n**AC-4.5:** The implementor returns a structured report suitable for orchestration and later verification\n\n- **TC-4.5a: Structured implementation result**\n  - Given: Story implementation and self-review complete\n  - When: The CLI returns the implementor result\n  - Then: The result includes identity fields, planning summary, changed files, tests added or changed, gates run, self-review findings, open questions, any spec deviations, and a recommended next step\n\n### Technical Design\n<!-- Jira: Technical Notes or sub-section of Description -->\n\n**Relevant Data Contracts**\n\n#### CLI Operations\n\n| Operation | Purpose | Required Inputs | Expected Outputs | Outcome States |\n|-----------|---------|-----------------|------------------|----------------|\n| Launch story implementor | Start story implementation | story id, role config, prompt assembly inputs, working directory | structured implementor result, provider id, session id | ready-for-verification, needs-followup-fix, needs-human-ruling, blocked |\n| Continue story implementor | Continue the retained implementor session | provider id, session id, follow-up prompt contract | continued implementor result | ready-for-verification, needs-followup-fix, needs-human-ruling, blocked |\n\n#### Implementor Result\n\n| Field | Description |\n|-------|-------------|\n| provider id | Provider/runtime used for the implementor |\n| session id | Identifier required to continue the same implementor session |\n| story id and title | Story identity |\n| plan summary | ACs, TCs, non-TC tests, approach, likely failure modes |\n| change inventory | Files changed and why |\n| test summary | Tests added, modified, removed, and total count after story |\n| test-count delta | Change in test total from the prior accepted story baseline |\n| gates run and gate results | What was executed and what passed or failed |\n| self-review findings fixed | Issues found and fixed during the self-review loop |\n| self-review findings surfaced | Uncertain or unresolved issues sent back to the orchestrator |\n| open questions | Blocking uncertainties |\n| spec deviations | Any divergence from story, tech design, or test plan |\n| recommended next step | Orchestrator routing recommendation |\n\n**Relevant Tech Design Sections**\n\n- `tech-design-cli-runtime.md` -> Flow 2: Story Implementation and Continuation, Continuation Handle Contract, Implementor Result Contract, Self-Review Rules, Provider Adapters\n- `tech-design-skill-process.md` -> Flow 4: Process Playbook and Command Routing\n- `test-plan.md` -> Prompt assembly, story implementor, provider adapter, and result-contract suites\n\n*See valid fixture | structured `validatedConfig` returned |\n\n## Flow 2: Story Implementation and Continuation\n\n**Covers:** AC-3.1, AC-3.2, AC-3.4a, AC-4.1 to AC-4.5\n\n`story-implement` launches the implementor for one story using the assembled prompt and the configured role assignment. The CLI owns prompt assembly, provider invocation, self-review prompt evolution, and the returned structured result. It does not own story acceptance. `story-continue` exists because the story implementor may keep working in the same provider session across follow-up fixes or post-review continuation.\n\nThe most important boundary here is continuity. The CLI may continue a provider session, but it must do so only from explicit continuation/session handles emitted in the previous result and handed back by the orchestrator. The CLI should never try to recover the “latest” session implicitly.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Orchestrator\n    participant CLI\n    participant Prompt as Prompt Assembly\n    participant Adapter as Provider Adapter\n    participant Harness as Secondary or Primary Harness\n\n    Note over Orchestrator,Harness: AC-3.1 to AC-4.5\n    Orchestrator->>CLI: story-implement --story-id <id>\n    CLI->>Prompt: Build implementor prompt\n    Prompt-->>CLI: assembled prompt + manifest\n    CLI->>Adapter: run implementor role\n    Adapter->>Harness: execute prompt\n    Harness-->>Adapter: result + session handle\n    CLI->>Prompt: run self-review pass 1..N\n    CLI-->>Orchestrator: structured implementor result + continuation handle\n```\n\n### Continuation Handle Contract\n\n```typescript\nexport interface ContinuationHandle {\n  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n  sessionId: string;\n  storyId: string;\n}\n```\n\n### Implementor Result Contract\n\n```typescript\nexport interface ImplementorResult {\n  resultId: string;\n  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n  model: string;\n  role: \"story_implementor\";\n  sessionId?: string;\n  outcome:\n    | \"ready-for-verification\"\n    | \"needs-followup-fix\"\n    | \"needs-human-ruling\"\n    | \"blocked\";\n  continuation?: ContinuationHandle;\n  story: {\n    id: string;\n    title: string;\n  };\n  planSummary: string;\n  changedFiles: Array<{ path: string; reason: string }>;\n  tests: {\n    added: string[];\n    modified: string[];\n    removed: string[];\n    totalAfterStory?: number;\n    deltaFromPriorBaseline?: number;\n  };\n  gatesRun: Array<{ command: string; result: \"pass\" | \"fail\" | \"not-run\" }>;\n  selfReview: {\n    passesRun: number;\n    findingsFixed: string[];\n    findingsSurfaced: string[];\n  };\n  openQuestions: string[];\n  specDeviations: string[];\n  recommendedNextStep: string;\n}\n```\n\n### Self-Review Rules\n\nThe CLI should own the mechanical shape of self-review:\n\n- Pass 1: broad correctness and obvious omissions\n- Pass 2: test/coverage and requirement alignment\n- Pass 3: residual risk and boundary audit\n\nStop rules:\n\n- stop early if the provider reports a blocking condition\n- stop early if a pass surfaces only human-ruling issues\n- otherwise run the configured number of passes\n\n### TC Mapping for This Flow\n\n| TC | Tests | Module | Setup | Assert |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| TC-3.1a | implementor prompt assembly uses base + snippets | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | implementor input fixture | assembly includes correct base prompt and snippets |\n| TC-3.1b | verifier prompt assembly uses base + snippets | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | verifier input fixture | verifier assets selected correctly |\n| TC-3.2a | implementor insert applied when present | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | insert file fixture | insert content included every run |\n| TC-3.2b | implementor insert omitted cleanly when absent | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | no insert file | no failure path and no insert ID |\n| TC-3.4a | implementor reading journey included | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | implementor input | reading-journey snippet and paths included |\n| TC-4.1a | story implement uses documented contract | `story-implement-command.test.ts` | valid config + story fixture | command returns structured implementor result |\n| TC-4.2a | self-review remains in same session | `provider-adapter.test.ts` | fake provider session | self-review calls reuse same session |\n| TC-4.2b | continuation handle returned | `story-implement-command.test.ts` | fake provider response with session | structured continuation returned |\n| TC-4.3a | configured number of self-review passes run | `story-implement-command.test.ts` | passes=3 config | three passes unless blocking stop |\n| TC-4.3b | self-review prompt evolves by pass | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | self-review fixtures | pass-specific snippets differ |\n| TC-4.4a | clear local fixes can be applied automatically | `story-implement-command.test.ts` | provider returns noncontroversial fix list | result records auto-applied fixes |\n| TC-4.4b | uncertain fix is surfaced | `story-implement-command.test.ts` | provider flags uncertainty | result outcome `needs-human-ruling` or surfaced finding |\n| TC-4.5a | implementor result has full contract | `result-contracts.test.ts` | valid result object | schema accepts required fields only |\n\n## Flow 3: Story Verification and Quick Fix\n\n**Covers:** AC-3.3, AC-3.4b, AC-3.4c, AC-5.1 to AC-5.5\n\n`story-verify` launches fresh verifiers by default. They should not reuse prior verifier sessions. `quick-fix` is intentionally lighter than a full implementor pass. It exists for small, bounded corrections that do not justify re-running the full story implementation workflow.\n\nThe orchestration rule here is simple. The CLI can execute verification or a quick fix, but it cannot decide that a story is accepted. It only returns disciplined findings and outcomes. The orchestrator still interprets them and runs the final gate.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Orchestrator\n    participant CLI\n    participant Adapter1 as Verifier Adapter A\n    participant Adapter2 as Verifier Adapter B\n    participant QuickFix as Quick Fix Adapter\n\n    Note over Orchestrator,QuickFix: AC-5.1 to AC-5.5\n    Orchestrator->>CLI: story-verify --story-id <id>\n    CLI->>Adapter1: launch verifier 1\n    CLI->>Adapter2: launch verifier 2\n    Adapter1-->>CLI: verifier result\n    Adapter2-->>CLI: verifier result\n    CLI-->>Orchestrator: verifier batch\n    Orchestrator->>CLI: quick-fix --request ...\n    CLI->>QuickFix: run bounded correction\n    QuickFix-->>CLI: quick-fix result\n    CLI-->>Orchestrator: quick-fix result\n```\n\n### Story Verifier Result Contract\n\n```typescript\nexport interface StoryVerifierResult {\n  resultId: string;\n  outcome: \"pass\" | \"revise\" | \"block\";\n  verifierLabel: string;\n  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n  model: string;\n  story: {\n    id: string;\n    title: string;\n  };\n  artifactsRead: string[];\n  reviewScopeSummary: string;\n  findings: VerifierFinding[];\n  requirementCoverage: {\n    verified: string[];\n    unverified: string[];\n  };\n  gatesRun: Array<{ command: string; result: \"pass\" | \"fail\" | \"not-run\" }>;\n  mockOrShimAuditFindings: string[];\n  recommendedFixScope:\n    | \"same-session-implementor\"\n    | \"quick-fix\"\n    | \"fresh-fix-path\"\n    | \"human-ruling\";\n  openQuestions: string[];\n  additionalObservations: string[];\n}\n```\n\n```typescript\nexport type FindingSeverity = \"critical\" | \"major\" | \"minor\" | \"observation\";\nexport type RequirementId = string;\n\nexport interface VerifierFinding {\n  id: string;\n  severity: FindingSeverity;\n  title: string;\n  evidence: string;\n  affectedFiles: string[];\n  requirementIds: RequirementId[];\n  recommendedFixScope:\n    | \"same-session-implementor\"\n    | \"quick-fix\"\n    | \"fresh-fix-path\"\n    | \"human-ruling\";\n  blocking: boolean;\n}\n```\n\n### Quick Fix Result Contract\n\n```typescript\nexport interface QuickFixResult {\n  resultId: string;\n  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n  model: string;\n  outcome:\n    | \"ready-for-verification\"\n    | \"needs-more-routing\"\n    | \"blocked\";\n  filesChanged: string[];\n  changeSummary: string;\n  gatesRun: Array<{ command: string; result: \"pass\" | \"fail\" | \"not-run\" }>;\n  unresolvedConcerns: string[];\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

root | Durable orchestration record and recovery state |\n| Structured command result JSON | CLI | stdout / orchestrator ingest | Machine-readable completion surface for each operation |\n| Prompt-ready execution payloads | CLI | secondary harnesses | Stable provider-facing prompt contract |\n| Bundled CLI artifact | build surface | packaged skill output | Runtime executable shipped with the skill |\n\n#### Outcome States\n\nThe CLI should use explicit outcome states instead of ambiguous free-text status messages.\n\n| Operation Class | Allowed Outcome States |\n|---|---|\n| `inspect`, `preflight` | `ready`, `needs-user-decision`, `blocked` |\n| implementation / quick-fix operations | `ready-for-verification`, `needs-followup-fix`, `needs-human-ruling`, `blocked` |\n| verifier batches | `pass`, `revise`, `block` |\n| cleanup operations | `cleaned`, `needs-more-cleanup`, `blocked` |\n| synthesis operations | `ready-for-closeout`, `needs-fixes`, `needs-more-verification`, `blocked` |\n\n#### Runtime Prerequisites\n\n| Prerequisite | Where Needed | How to Verify |\n|---|---|---|\n| Bun 1.3.x+ | local development, build, tests | `bun --version` |\n| Node-compatible runtime | packaged CLI execution | `node -v` |\n| Git repository root | CLI commands that inspect repo/worktree context | `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` |\n| `codex` CLI | only
